sprint: Object Strip Label + Zero-Holder Layout fixes
task: Fixed two real, user-reported Builder->Creator gaps found authoring a real World: (1) an Object Strip labeling bug -- tools/world-builder-v2's convergeSceneLayer() never carried a Scene Layer's own author-given name into the compiled Layer Pack entry's label field, so every Layer on one Scene fell back to humanizing the shared scene-<id>-<layerId> compiled id, reading as visually-identical truncated 'Scene Sin...' cards; fixed by carrying layer.name through as label. (2) a real 'no Holder' gap -- a Scene converged with zero Places had no way to signal that to Creator (both it and 'one Place, no Frame chosen' produced defaultFrame:null), because the compiled Layout never populated the holders field Sprint 9.6 had reserved for exactly this; convergeScene() now emits the real holders count, and renderer/slideRenderer.js's _resolveBorder / js/objectStrip.js's Artwork Place card both skip fabricating a picture area when holders===0, while staying null-safe (absent field = unchanged behaviour) for every existing theme including Museum Gallery's own hand-authored layouts. Investigation also confirmed the actual pixel z-order was already correct -- a Scene's Background fill already paints first, behind the Frame/Panel/every Place. Verified via a synthetic two-Layout Playwright test plus builder.js's real convergeScene/convergeSceneLayer driven directly against hand-built Scenes. Full existing regression suite and goldenBuild.js (30/30) pass unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
